findings.description: Title and author information are duplicated on the first page: the styled slide header area displays the title, presenter name, division, and date, and then the same content is repeated immediately below as plain HTML text headings and paragraphs.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the title '15A NCAC 02K .0224 - Additional Requirements for Dams that Impound Coal Combustion Residuals' and 'Andrew Brooks, PE / State Dam Safety Engineer…/ September 4, 2018' once. The HTML page 1 shows these in the styled banner area between the two photos AND again as an H1 heading and body text beneath the cityscape photo.

findings.description: Three literal URLs/email addresses that were clickable hyperlinks in the PDF appear as underlined plain text in the HTML without <a href> wrapping.
findings.evidence: PDF page 8 shows clickable hyperlinks for: (1) 'https://deq.nc.gov/documents/15a-ncac-02k-0224-additional-requirements-dams-impound-coal-combustion-residual', (2) 'Andrew.brooks@ncdenr.gov', (3) 'daniel.kang@ncdenr.gov'. In the HTML, all three appear inside <u> tags as plain text without any <a href> wrapper.

findings.description: An image with empty alt text (alt="") appears to be a substantive photograph (city skyline at dusk, likely Asheville, NC) from the bottom of PDF page 1, not a purely decorative element.
findings.evidence: HTML: — The PDF shows a large photograph of a city skyline at dusk, which is a substantive image deserving descriptive alt text rather than empty alt.
